guide.title: Cultivation Guide
guide.sub: Follow the 3-stage system to grow Cordyceps Militaris
guide.stage1p: Develop pure and stable starter culture
s1step1c: Prepare a nutrient-rich base using potato extract, dextrose, and agar
Ensure proper mixing for uniform gel-like consistency
Pour into sterile containers before solidification
s1step2c: Maintain hygienic conditions to eliminate contaminants
Use controlled heat and pressure for purity
s1step3c: Observe for contamination in first 3-5 days
s1step4c: Introduce small, healthy culture sample at center
s1step6c: Introduce light exposure at later stage
Observe color transformation: White → Yellow → Orange
guide.s1f: Pure base culture ready for further multiplication
s2step1c: Prepare using: Potato broth, Dextrose, Peptone, Yeast extract, Magnesium sulfate, Vitamins (B1, B12)
Mix thoroughly to ensure uniform distribution
s2step2c: Proper sealing is critical for success
s2step3c: Allow medium to cool naturally to room temperature
s2step4c: Transfer small portion from PDA culture
Place culture into liquid medium carefully
s2step6c: Perform periodic gentle shaking to distribute nutrients
Avoid excessive disturbance after initial phase
s2step7c: Introduce light near the final stage (day 18-20)
Observe color change: White → Yellow → Orange
guide.s2f: Liquid spawn ready for large-scale cultivation
s3step1c: Combine with basal nutrient solution in proper ratio
s3step3c: Allow substrate to cool before inoculation
s3step4c: Add liquid spawn into substrate evenly
s3step6c: Gradually introduce light exposure in stages
s3step8c: Total growth period: 56–60 days from inoculation
Harvest when fruiting bodies reach full maturity
guide.s3f: Fully developed Cordyceps militaris ready for harvest

dash.a1: Temperature is high → Reduce heat or increase ventilation
dash.a2: Humidity is low → Add moisture or increase misting
